Items.Title: 我参加现代前端大会的经历 | My experience of Modern Frontends Conference
Items.Description:
今年 6 月，Modern Frontends 会议联系了我。 就我而言，这是另一个希望我发言的感兴趣的会议。 在推特上与会议联系后，我通过 Sessionize 提交了 TBC 演讲标题和描述，并被接受发言。 这本身并不完全不寻常，但有点奇怪，因为我当时不知道会议的幕后黑手是谁。 他们通过“现代前端”的推特句柄在推特上联系了我，并没有签署任何人的消息，在网站上做一些调查并没有带来任何澄清。 我猜想这一定是认识我的人，并且凭信心接受了我空白的 CFP。

后来我发现 Modern Frontends 的组织者是 Gen Ashley，她提到她有一个团队与她一起工作，但没有透露他们的名字，也没有表扬他们的工作。

现代前端随后向我工作的公司寻求赞助，我们最终因冲突而拒绝赞助。

决定在 Modern Frontends 上发言对我来说很容易，我住在会议区域，而且我已经准备好了演讲。 我的工作是开发者宣传，所以我写作和发表演讲的时间由我的薪水支付。

然而并不是每个人都这样，我听说有出差的演讲者不仅没有旅行保险，而且也没有酒店。

让演讲者，尤其是新演讲者付费参加会议并在会议上发言是不可接受的。 支付旅行费用和支付酒店费用算作支付参加费用。 演讲者就是内容，没有他们就没有会议。 补偿发言者的时间。 我什至需要说这件事，这让我感到非常奇怪。 会议至少应该做的是支付差旅和住宿费用，提供演讲者津贴是会议应该做的。

因为我在 DevRel 工作，根据合同，我经常有义务不收取演讲费，通常，我会要求将其捐赠给多样性门票和奖学金，但当会议以其他方式补偿我的时间和精力时，我很感激. 我在很多会议上演讲过，通常他们会为他们的演讲者做一些好事，比如在酒店房间里送礼物，或者参观当地，或者举办演讲者晚宴，或者以上的组合。 例如，NDC 以演讲者的名义向慈善机构捐款。

我看到很多人在网上发表上述言论，实际上被阿什利将军压制、关闭、羞辱和欺负，不得不撤回和删除他们的帖子。 她对演讲者、赞助商和任何叫她出去的人都很粗鲁。 在活动中退出演讲的演讲者仍然在会议网站和相关广告上贴满了他们的脸。

当我到达活动本身时，它非常严峻，很难找到场地，没有品牌，灯火通明。 它给人一种企业地带光地毯瓷砖地狱的感觉。 赞助商在走廊周围摆放着桌子，显然对布置和活动本身不以为然。 赞助商是在 3000 名与会者身上出售的，活动中肯定没有 3000 人，也许只有几百人。

我已经公开表达了我对开发者活动中显示的过度和权利感到不安，免费食物、衣服和其他赃物被分发给已经幸运和位置优越的人。 然而，这一事件将事情推向了另一个极端。 以 600 英镑的门票价格，与会者可以享用单一的咖啡服务，包括预制咖啡和茶的罐子以及“包三明治”午餐。 它也在一个小时内被扫地出门，所以迟到的与会者或那些在午休时间工作的人没有得到午餐。 我不想听起来有权利，但赞助的价格和赞助商的数量，以及门票的价格，以及他们没有支付演讲者费用的事实（我假设他们可能已经支付了一些更大的名字）感觉这些资金已经花在了其他地方......

资金没有花在录制和直播会谈上。 任何一个房间都没有录音设备。 当我问 Gen 直播发生了什么事时，她只是告诉我，她将退还那些支付了直播门票的人，并为他们提供明年活动的免费门票（哈！）。 在我询问的时候，现场直播的门票仍然可以在活动网站上以 43 英镑的价格购买。 这对我来说非常接近欺诈。 我知道人们一直在网上要求退款，我希望他们能尽快得到退款。 我希望如果明年举办该活动，它会得到更好的管理。

我发表了演讲，一切顺利，房间里没有人为我做准备，只有靠在讲台上的 AV 设置。 我让自己站起来，发表演讲，然后离开了房间。 当我穿过走廊时，Gen 走过来告诉我，她认为人们在 Twitter 上抱怨这件事很“有趣”，她不在乎。 我真的不知道该怎么回应，所以我说这不是真的好笑而且看起来很严肃然后就这样了。

关于这次会议，我要说的最后一点是，它被安排在每年 HalfStack London 举行的同一天举行。 近 10 年来，Dylan 每年都在同一天举办一场绝对出色、包容和富有创意的前端会议。 我知道其他人已经分享了他们的经历，我不想把自己集中在这场灾难中，但我也不愿意眼睁睁地看着这一切发生而不说话。 我一直提倡技术的公平和多样性，我会继续这样做。 如果我参与这次会议和迄今为止的沉默伤害了任何人，我深表歉意。



原文:

I was reached out to by Modern Frontends conference back in June this year. November is the most busy month of my DevRel calendar, I always expect to be travelling and speaking a lot around this time. As far as I was concerned, this was another interested conference who wanted me to speak. I submitted via Sessionize, after being reached out to by the conference on twitter, with a TBC talk title and description and was accepted to speak. This by itself isn’t completely unusual, but it was a little odd as I didn’t at that point know who was behind the conference. They had reached out to me on twitter via a ‘modern frontends’ twitter handle and didn’t sign off their messages from anyone, doing some sleuthing on the website didn’t bring any clarification. I assumed that it must be someone who knows me and had accepted my blank CFP on faith.

I later discovered that the organiser of Modern Frontends is Gen Ashley, she mentions that she has a team working with her, but has not named them, nor given them credit for their work.

Modern frontends then reached out to the company I work at for sponsorship, we eventually declined to sponsor due to clashes.

Deciding to speak at Modern Frontends was easy for me, I live in the area of the conference and I already have prepared talks. My job is in developer advocacy, so my time writing and giving the talk is covered by my salary.

However this is not the case for everyone, I have heard that speakers who had to travel not only didn’t have their travel covered, but no hotel either.

Making speakers, especially new speakers, pay to attend and speak at your conference is unacceptable. Paying for travel and paying for a hotel counts as paying to attend. The speakers are the content, without them there is no conference. Compensate speakers for their time. It feels absolutely bizarre to me that this even needs to be said. ‘Networking opportunities at the event’ or ‘exposure’ do not count as compensation, as they say, people die of exposure. The least a conference should do is cover travel and accommodation, offering a speaker stipend is what conferences should do.

Because I work in DevRel, I am often contractually obliged not to take a speaker fee, usually, I will ask that it be donated to diversity tickets and scholarships, but I do appreciate when a conference compensates me for my time and effort in other ways. I have spoken at many conferences and usually they will do something nice for their speakers, like a gift in the hotel room, or a tour of the local area, or a speaker dinner or combination of the above. NDC gives a donation to a charity in the speakers’ name, for example.

I have seen multiple people online making statements to the above effect and being virtually silenced, shut down, shamed and bullied by Gen Ashley into retracting and deleting their posts. She has been rude to speakers, sponsors and anyone who has called her out. Speakers who have stood down from speaking at the event still have their faces plastered all over the conference website and associated advertising.

When I arrived at the event itself it was pretty grim, the venue was hard to find, unbranded, starkly lit. It was giving corporate-strip-light-carpet-tiled-hell vibes. The sponsors had tables around the hallway and were clearly unimpressed with the setup and the event itself. Sponsors were sold on 3000 attendees, there were certainly not 3000 people at the event, perhaps a few hundred.

I have spoken openly about my discomfort around the excess and entitlement that is shown at developer events, with free food, clothing and other swag being given out to the already fortunate and well situated. However this event took things to the other extreme. For the price of a £600 ticket, attendees were treated to a single coffee service, with urns of pre-made coffee and tea and a lunch of ‘packet sandwiches’. It was swept away within an hour too, so late attendees or those working over their lunch-break did not get a lunch. I don’t want to sound entitled, but for the price of the sponsorship and the quantity of sponsors, along with the price of the tickets, and the fact that they weren’t paying their speakers (I assume they might have paid some of the bigger names) it feels like the funds have been spent elsewhere…

Where the funds weren’t spent was on recording and livestreaming the talks. There was no recording equipment in any of the rooms. When I asked Gen about what had happened to the livestream she just told me that she was going to refund the people who had paid for livestream tickets and offer them a free ticket to next year’s event (ha!). At the time I asked, tickets for the livestream were still available for purchase on the event website at £43. This feels very close to fraud to me. I know people have been asking online for refunds, I hope they get them soon. I hope that if the event runs next year it is better managed.

I gave my talk and it went well, there was no one in the room to set me up, just an AV setup leaning against the podium. I set myself, up, gave the talk and left the room. As I walked through the hallway Gen came up to me and told me that she thought it was ‘funny’ that people were complaining about the event on twitter, and that she didn’t care. I really didn’t know what to respond to this, so I said that it wasn’t really funny and seemed quite serious and left it at that.

My final point to make about this conference is that it was booked to run on the same day that HalfStack London is on every year. Dylan has been running an absolutely excellent, inclusive and creative front end focused conference for almost 10 years on the same day every year. Modern Frontends booked to run on exactly the same day, which shows either rudeness or a lack of market research. Dylan ended up moving the conference to run on the Wednesday before Modern Frontends ran and it was a roaring success.

I know others have shared their experiences, and I do not want to center myself in this debacle, but it also does not sit well with me to watch this unfold and say nothing. I always advocate for fairness and diversity in tech and I will keep doing so. I apologise if my involvement in this conference and silence up until now has hurt anyone.

Items.Title: Speech Racer：我的第一个黑客马拉松项目 | Speech Racer: My first hackathon project
Items.Description:
我参加了我的第一次黑客马拉松！ 这很有趣，我以前参加过黑客马拉松，但我从来没有参加过。 这是Deepgram的内部黑客马拉松，我们与来自不同团队的人配对。 我们的团队由我 (DevRel)、工程部的 Nik、Brian 和 Chris、产品部的 Nick 和增长营销部的 Alexa 组成。 我以前曾以某种身份与我的几个团队成员一起工作过，但没有那么多，也没有在任何充满压力的情况下工作过。 闲聊的时间不多了，我们开始快速交谈，开始了整个晚上的头脑风暴会议，在我们的梦中，然后神奇地登陆谷歌文档进行早上的讨论……好吧，好吧，那不是它到底是怎么发生的，但你明白了。 我们很快就开始做生意了。

想法

我们想出了很多想法，分布在各种类别中，包括将 Deepgram 用于社会公益（想想教育、救灾、心理健康），将 Deepgram 用于非社会公益（抢劫、窃取商业机密，你知道的，正常事物）、物联网实施、游戏（Flappy Bird，有人吗？）和假日主题项目。

然后有两个

所以我们将范围缩小到最后两个项目。

语速快

一种衡量说话速度和准确性的游戏，类似于打字游戏，例如https://typings.gg/ 。 我们可以设计一些有趣的挑战，比如尝试快速准确地说出绕口令、难说的单词、随机单词、歌词、名言。 我们可以添加不同语言的挑战。

  • 有趣、互动、可共享且易于尝试/理解。 有时，这些类型的游戏/挑战确实会在Hackernews上流行起来。
  • 要求 Deepgram ASR API 具有无与伦比的速度和准确性。

历史专业

  • 转录口述历史 - 包括主题检测、总结和可视化。 如果我们能找到一个用文本创建可视化的好引语，那就太好了。
  • 使用 Deepgram 口述历史起源故事、主题检测对它们进行分组，然后生成说话者的超级英雄图像作为其起源故事的卡片。
  • 对于未来的员工来说，看到 Deepgrammers 谈论的内容并在我们的网站上展示我们的技术可能会很酷。 我们可以添加一个包含热门主题的可视化效果，您可以单击它来查看口述历史。

获胜者，冠军

要玩游戏，请点击“播放”按钮。 会有一个倒计时，然后会出现文字。 尽可能快速准确地阅读单词，然后单击“完成”按钮。 下面是一个 gif，您可以体验一下这款游戏，但您绝对应该现在就去 speechracer.com玩一下。

执行

我们必须快速选择一个技术栈。 我们选择了 React、Tailwind，并将其托管为 S3 存储桶中的静态网站。

旁白：我以前从未使用过 Tailwind，这是一次有趣的学习经历。 我知道我绝对没有在这里使用最佳实践，但是这里有很多类。 感谢 Brandon Coyer 的博客文章Tailwind Gradients - How to Make a Glowing Gradient Background 。 我不是一个设计师，但我是渐变的忠实粉丝——正如你在项目中看到的那样。 我确实想知道 Tailwind 有多么强大，它可能是我见过的最好的文档。 从来没有一次我无法在文档中找到我需要的东西。 所以大声喊出 Tailwind 文档的人。

因为这是一项快速而肮脏的工作，所以我们的树枝飞扬。 对于这个项目，游戏会给出一个引语，玩家必须尽可能快和准确地说出引语，然后你就会得到你的结果。 为此，我们需要麦克风访问、websocket 实施，以将给出的引语与口头记录相匹配，然后计算每分钟的单词数、准确性和持续时间。 我们还添加了三个不同的类别供玩家选择。

一点代码

如果你想跟进这个项目，你可以在https://github.com/deepgram-devs/speechracer克隆 repo。

要运行，您需要自己的Deepgram API 密钥。 别担心，您的 api 密钥会让您在游戏中享受很多小时的乐趣。

要开始游戏，用户选择播放按钮，这会产生倒计时。 为此，我们调用 countdown 函数，它倒数 3-2-1，将播放设置为 true，从而触发新布局。

 function play() { setCountdown(3); setTimeout(() => { setPlaying(true); startNewChallenge(); }, 2750); }

因为用户可以从三个不同的挑战中进行选择，它们都是以相同的方式实现的，所以我们为每个挑战提供了使用相同方法的功能。 我们从一个导入的 json 文件中抓取了文本，然后选择了一个随机引用：

 function getTongueTwister() { return tongueTwisters[Math.floor(Math.random() * tongueTwisters.length)]; }

挑战

为了确保计算的准确性，我们必须确保我们消除了引号开头和结尾的空格，不计算大写，并删除了标点符号。 为此，我们创建了一个processText函数：

 function processText(s) { return s .trim() .toLowerCase() .replace(/[,'.:;?]/g, ""); }

未来特色

这是一场黑客马拉松，所以我们显然没有时间实现我们想要的所有功能。 值得注意的是我们要添加的一些功能——我们是一个开源项目，因此欢迎您创建问题或提交 PR 😉

  • 添加复活节彩蛋。 例如，如果玩家击中了一定的精度，就会弹出一些东西。 如果我正在做 Mariah Carey 的 All I want for Christmas is you，并且达到 100%，那将是最大的回报。 应该有升级、尝试不同类别等的激励措施。
  • 不同语言
  • 排行榜
  • 跟踪您自己的统计数据
  • 主题（例如深色模式、假日）
  • 用户提交成绩单
  • 关键字提升
  • 进展（逐渐困难的提示）

黑客马拉松外卖

我可能会在另一篇文章中对此进行更多介绍，但事实证明这将是一次非常有趣的体验。 我以前从未真正在这样的团队工作过。 当你与其他人快速合作时，你会学到很多关于沟通、实施和任务生成的知识。 在某些方面，这会让事情变得更难，但也会挑战你走出舒适区，你会因此而得到回报。 但你为什么还在读书？ 去玩语音赛车！ 哦，你可以查看所有 Deepgram 项目并评论、投票、分享我们的黑客马拉松讨论。



原文:

I participated in my first hackathon! It's funny, I've run hackathons before, but I've never been a part of one. This is an internal hackathon for Deepgram, and we were paired with people from different teams. Our team consisted of me (DevRel), Nik, Brian, and Chris from Engineering, Nick from Product, and Alexa from Growth Marketing. I've worked with a couple of my team members in some capacity before, but not this much or under any sort of pressure-filled situation. With little time for small talk, we got to fast talk and kicked off the brain storming session that went through the night, in our dreams, and landed magically in a google doc for morning discussion...ok, well, that wasn't exactly how it happened, but you get the point. We got down to business quickly.

The Ideas

We came up with quite a few ideas, spread out over a variety of categories, including Using Deepgram for Social Good (think education, disaster relief, mental health), Using Deepgram for not Social good (heists, stealing trade secrets, you know, normal things), Internet of Things implementation, games (Flappy Bird, anyone?), and holiday theme projects.

And then there were two

So we narrowed it down to the final two projects.

FastTalker

A game to measure speaking speed and accuracy, similar to typing games like this one https://typings.gg/. We could build some fun challenges like trying to quickly and accurately speak tongue twisters, difficult-to-say words, random words, song lyrics, famous quotes. We could add challenges in different languages.

  • A be fun, interactive, shareable, and quick to try/understand. Sometimes these types of games/challenges do trend on Hackernews.
  • Requires that the Deepgram ASR API has unmatched speed and accuracy.

HistoryMajor

  • Transcribe Oral histories - include topic detection, summarization, and visualization. It would be cool if we could id a good quote that created a visualization with text.
  • Use Deepgram oral history origin stories, topic detection to group them and then generate superhero images of the speaker as the card for their origin story.
  • Spin: careers-page content that combines oral histories from employees with topic detection. It could be cool for prospective employees to see what Deepgrammers talk about and also to showcase our technology on our website. We could add a visualization with top topics that you can click on to see oral histories.

The Winner

FastTalker won by a thread and evolved into Speech Racer.

To play the game, you hit the Play button. There will be a countdown, and then words will appear. Read the words as quickly and as accurately as possible and then click the Finish button. (Did I mention you can play it on mobile?)

Below is a gif so you can get a taste of the game, but you definitely should go play it RIGHT NOW over at speechracer.com.

Implementation

We had to quickly choose a tech stack. We went with React, Tailwind, and hosted this as a static website out of an S3 bucket.

Aside: I had never used Tailwind before and it was an interesting learning experience. I know that I definitely am not using best practices here, but there's an awful lot of classes here. Shoutout to Brandon Coyer for his blog post Tailwind Gradients - How to Make a Glowing Gradient Background. I'm not much of a designer, but I'm a big fan of gradients--as you can see in the project. I do want to know however overwhelming Tailwind felt, it has maybe the best docs I've ever seen. There was never a time that I wasn't able to find what I needed in the docs. So shoutout the the Tailwind docs folks.

Because this was a fast and dirty job, we had branches flying. For this project, the game would drop a quote, the player would have to say the quote as fast and as accurately as possible and then you would get your results. To do that, we needed microphone access, websocket implementation, to match the quote given with the spoken transcript and then calculate words per minute, accuracy, and duration. We also added three different categories the player could choose from.

A little bit of the Code

If you want to follow along with this project, you can clone the repo at https://github.com/deepgram-devs/speechracer.

To get things running, you'll need your own Deepgram API Key. Don't worry, your api key will get you many hours of fun with the game.

To start the game off, the user chooses the play button, which generates a countdown. To do that, we call the countdown function, which counts down 3-2-1, sets playing to true, which triggers the new layout.

function play() {

setCountdown(3);

setTimeout(() => {

setPlaying(true);

startNewChallenge();

}, 2750);

}

Because a user could choose from three different challenges, which were all implemented the same way, we had functions for each that used the same approach. We grabbed the text from an imported json file, and then chose a random quote:

function getTongueTwister() {

return tongueTwisters[Math.floor(Math.random() * tongueTwisters.length)];

}

Challenges

To make sure that the accuracy was calculated right, we had to make sure that we eliminated white space from the beginning and end of quotes, didn't count capitalization, and removed punctuation. To do that, we created a processText function:

function processText(s) {

return s

.trim()

.toLowerCase()

.replace(/[,'.:;?]/g, "");

}

Future Features

It's a hackathon, so we obviously didn't have time to get all the features implemented that we want to. It's worth noting some of the features we'd like to add-we're an open source project, so you're welcome to create an issue or submit a PR 😉

  • Add Easter eggs. For example, if the player hits a certain accuracy, something will pop up. If I were doing Mariah Carey's All I want for Christmas is you, and hit 100%, it would give the biggest reward. There should be incentives to level up, try different categories, etc.
  • Different languages
  • Leaderboard
  • Track your own statistics
  • Themes (e.g. dark mode, holiday)
  • User submitted transcripts
  • Keyword boosting
  • Progression (gradually harder prompts)

Hackathon Takeaways

I'll probably write more about this in a different post, but this turned out to be a pretty fun experience. I haven't really worked on a team like this before. You learn a lot about communication, implementation, and task generation when you're working quickly with other people. In some ways that makes things harder, but also challenges you to get out of your comfort zone and you're rewarded for that. But why are you still reading? Go play Speech Racer! Oh, and you can check all the Deepgram projects and comment, upvote, share on our hackathon discussion.

懒惰的还是急切的流？ 懒人流热切流

术语

  • 单播- 每个流只有一个订阅者，新订阅创建新流
  • 多播- 每个流没有或有很多订阅者，新订阅使用现有流
  • 惰性流- 只有当有人开始订阅它时才开始发出值
  • 科特林流

    通过创建Flow并调用FlowCollector<T>.emit() ，在流内部生成Flow数据。 它被称为上游流。

     val flow : Flow < Int > = flow { repeat ( 10000 ) { value -> delay ( 1000 ) emit ( value ) } }

    但是，在调用Flow<T>.collect()之前不会发出数据。 这是在ViewModel中收集流的示例。 它被称为下游流。

     viewModelScope . launch { flow . collect { value -> /* collecting $value here */ } }

    每次调用Flow<T>collect()时，都会创建一个新的冷流。 它被称为单播。 数据仅在此时发出 -惰性流。

    科特林频道

    创建通道和将数据发送到热流是分开的。 向热流发送数据称为通道的发送方。

    创建频道

    private val channel = Channel < Int >()

    将数据发送到热流

    viewModelScope . launch { repeat ( 10000 ) { value -> delay ( 1000 ) channel . send ( value ) } }

    热流就像一个缓冲区，具有缓冲能力。 默认缓冲区容量为零。 默认情况下，当缓冲区溢出时，数据发送暂停，直到有接收者。

    要接收通道的数据，您可以调用Channel.receive() 。 这称为通道的接收器。

     viewModelScope . launch { while ( true ) { val value = channel . receive () } }

    • 由于默认情况下缓冲区容量为零，缓冲区溢出会在您将第一个数据发送到流中时立即发生。 因此，数据发送在第一个数据之后暂停，直到有接收者。 这种行为看起来与惰性流非常相似，但事实并非如此。 它仍然是一个渴望的流。

    • 想象一下，如果缓冲区容量大于零，即使没有任何接收器也会发送数据，直到缓冲区已满。 因此，它是一个急切的流。

    与Flow不同，拥有新订阅者或新频道的接收者不会创建新流。 它仍然使用现有的流。 因此，它是多播流。

    我想强调的一件事是一旦接收到数据，数据就会从热流/缓冲区中删除。

    根据上图，假设已经有频道的接收者#1、#2 和#3 订阅了频道（调用Channel.receive()函数），频道的发送者正在发送1 、 2和3到热流。

    • 一旦接收器# 1接收到 1，接收器#2 和#3 将不再接收1 。
    • 接收者#2 接收到2 ，接收者#3 接收到3 。

    结论

    我只是总结了在高层使用Flow和Channel的行为。 我真的不知道它们的任何实际用法，尤其是Channel 。

    这些是我写下的笔记：

    • 我一直在使用它来公开本地数据库中的数据。 但老实说，我们什么时候使用Channel ？ 小吃店？ 如果你对它有很好的实际使用，请与我分享。

      简而言之，将一切都与StateFlow使用——将在下一篇文章中介绍。

      • ViewModel：一次性事件反模式

原文:

Summarizing the behaviors of using Kotlin flows (cold stream) and Kotlin channels (hot stream)

This is part of the asynchronous flow series:

Kotlin Flow is cold stream, Kotlin Channel is hot stream. The differences between hot and cold streams are summarized below.

Cold Stream vs Hot Stream

Behaviors Cold Stream Hot Stream
Where data is produced? Inside the stream outside the stream
Unicast or multicast? Unicast Multicast
Lazy or eager stream? Lazy stream Eager stream

Terminology

  • Unicast - has only one subscriber per stream, new subscription creates new stream
  • Multicast - has no or many subscribers per stream, new subscription uses existing stream
  • Lazy stream - start emitting values only when someone start subscribing to it
  • Eager stream - start emitting values even it does NOT have any subscribers

Kotlin Flows

Flow data is produced inside the stream by creating the Flow and calling the FlowCollector<T>.emit(). It is called upstream flow.

val flow: Flow<Int> = flow { repeat(10000) { value -> delay(1000) emit(value) }
}

However, the data won't be emitted until the Flow<T>.collect() is called. This is an example of collecting flow in ViewModel. It is called downstream flow.

viewModelScope.launch { flow.collect { value -> /* collecting $value here */ } }

Each time the Flow<T>collect() is called, a new cold stream is created. It is called unicast. Data is only emitting at this point - lazy stream.

Kotlin Channels

Channel data is produced outside the stream. Creating the channel and sending the data into the hot stream are separated. Sending data to the hot stream is called channel's sender.

Create the Channel

private val channel = Channel<Int>()

Send Data into the Hot Stream

viewModelScope.launch { repeat(10000) { value -> delay(1000) channel.send(value) }
}

Hot stream is like a buffer which has the buffer capacity. The default buffer capacity is zero. By default, when the buffer is overflow, the data sending is suspended until there is a receiver.

To receive the channel's data, you call the Channel.receive(). This is called the channel's receiver.

viewModelScope.launch { while(true) { val value = channel.receive() }
}

  • Since the buffer capacity is zero by default, the buffer overflow happens as soon as you're sending the first data into the stream. Thus, the data sending is suspended after the first data until there is a receiver. This behavior looks very similar to lazy stream, but it is NOT. It is still an eager stream.

  • Imagine if the buffer capacity is more than zero, sending data occurs even without any receiver until the buffer is full. Thus, it is an eager stream.

Unlike Flow, having a new subcriber or new channel's receiver does not create a new stream. It still uses the existing stream. Thus, it is multicast stream.

One thing I would like to emphasize is once the data is received, the data is removed from the hot stream / buffer.

Based on the diagram above, assuming there are already channel's receiver #1, #2 and #3 subscribing to the channel (calling the Channel.receive()function), the channel's sender is sending 1, 2 and 3 to the hot stream.

  • Once 1 is received by receiver #1, receiver #2 and #3 won't receive 1 anymore.
  • Receiver #2 receives 2 instead, and receiver #3 receives 3.

Conclusion

I just summarize the behavior of using Flow and Channel at high-level. I do not really know any practical usages of them, especially Channel.

These are the notes that I have written down:

  • Flow - store single value that changes over time
  • Channel - store one-time event, collected by one observer?

Flow probably makes sense. I have been using it to expose the data from a local database. But honestly, when do we use Channel? Snackbar? If you have a good practical usage of it, please share that with me.

[Updated - Sept 23, 2022]: After doing some researches and found the following article by Google team which basically does NOT recommend Channel for one-time event. In short, use everything with StateFlow - will be covered in next article.

  • ViewModel: One-off event antipatterns

Source Code

GitHub Repository: Demo_AsyncFlow (see FlowActivity and ChannelActivity)

Diogo Neves

Recentemente, durante uma conversa em um grupo de amigos desenvolvedores, surgiu uma dúvida: Uma super IA poderia ou não “roubar” o espaço dos programadores no mercado de trabalho?

Para ter um parâmetro de quão “super” essa IA precisaria ser, imagine a combinação das ferramentas e IAs mais poderosas atualmente, exatamente aquelas que você está acostumado a usar, certamente seria muito poderosa.

A minha resposta...

Começo minha resposta com uma pergunta: atualmente, na empresa em que você trabalha, sendo júnior ou sênior, sua única responsabilidade é sentar em uma cadeira e programar?

Um programador é semelhante a um arquiteto e como tau, tem grande importância naquilo que está sendo construído, ele normalmente vai além do código, fazendo parte do planejamento, acompanhado execução e ajudando na tomada de decisões de acordo com as necessidades do projeto.

Conclusão

Eu vejo essas “ferramentas” literalmente como ferramentas que ajudam ou vão ajudar o programador a ser mais produtivo ou que simplesmente atacam um determinado problema que pode ser automatizado, mesmo com uma “Super AI“, não vejo o mundo dos programadores sendo abalado, de fato.

Na verdade, com a automatização de determinadas tarefas sobra mais tempo para os programadores focarem em coisas realmente importantes como qualidade do código, testes e novas funcionalidade. Ou seja, contribui para desenvolvermos softwares incríveis e torná-lo mais sustentáveis ao longo do tempo.

Deixe-me saber se concorda ou não comigo nos comentários!

Obrigado por chegar até aqui!

您可以在 Solidity 中声明一个数组。

 type [ length ] name = [ ... ];

每个元素在数组中都有一个索引（位置）。 数组可用于保存固定或未知的值集合，而无需为每个值创建不同的变量。

假设您有来自五个投票单位的选票统计。 您无需创建五个变量来保存这些投票计数，而是创建一个数组来保存它们。

 uint [ 5 ] votes = [ 10 , 20 , 8 , 88 , 14 ];

数组以类型、长度、标识符和值开头。

在上面的例子中，我们指定这个数组应该只包含无符号整数。 数组的长度指定数组可以容纳的总值。 在我们的示例中，我们将数组设置为仅包含五个值。

标识符是数组的名称。 只要给它任何有意义的名字。

值是数组中的值。

原文:

You can declare an array in Solidity.

type[length] name = [ ... ];

Arrays in Solidity are fixed collections of elements with the same data type values. Each element has an index(position) within the array. An array is useful for holding fixed or unknown collections of values without having to create a different variable for each value.

Let's say you have vote counts from five polling units. Instead of creating five variables to hold each of these votes count, you create a single array to hold them.

uint[5] votes = [10, 20, 8, 88, 14];

An array starts with the type, length, identifier, and values.

Type is the data type of the array. In the above example, we specify this array should contain only unsigned integers. Solidity support multiple types e.g string.

Length of the array specifies the total values that the array can hold. Our example we set the array to hold only five values.

Identifier is the name of the array. Just give it any name that makes sense.

Values are the values in the array.

Hope that helps!

Items.Title: 审查 PR | Reviewing PRs
Items.Description:
今天，我审查了对我们内部课程项目提出的多个合并请求。 我想为其中一个较新的项目做出贡献，所以我决定从 Seneca 的 VSCode 扩展开始。 另外，我真的很喜欢它可以变成什么样子的想法。

链接： https ://github.com/Seneca-CDOT/vscode-seneca-college/pull/13

我发现的第一个拉取请求是由@IvaniGabrovsky提出的，它包含与创建 CONTRIBUTING.md 和 README.md 文件相关的更改。

同时，README.md 的主要受众是用户，它应该说明该工具是什么、如何使用它以及任何其他有用的信息。 它也是了解该项目时的第一站。

我决定还是去看一看。 我在发表第一条评论后开始审查，并以更改请求结束。

我专注于多个领域，例如：

  1. 语法，因为这些文件代表项目并且不能有大的语法错误（影响声誉）；
  2. 措辞和内容，因为我已经阅读了许多其他项目的类似文件，所以我可以说出一些重要的步骤并可以提出建议；
  3. 其他意见的指导，因为 Ivan 有时会通过个人信息寻求帮助，我想尽可能地指导他，并就前面提到的问题提出一些建议。

链接： https ://github.com/Seneca-CDOT/vscode-seneca-college/pull/12

我继续审查同一个项目中的拉取请求并找到了这个，它是由@P-DR0ZD 提出的，专注于为项目设置 Gitpod。 它创建了主要的 Gitpod 文件并将有关 Gitpod 设置的信息添加到 README.md。

首先，我必须研究Gitpod ，因为我对它没有太多经验，为了测试它，我需要弄清楚预期的结果。 经过一些谷歌搜索并查看其文档后，我发现它允许设置开发环境以在线处理项目，这对于进行贡献非常有帮助。

首先，我检查了新 README.md 文件中提供的链接，它运行良好。 我可以通过 Gitpod 打开 VSCode，项目会自动为我准备好。

我还检查了新的Gitpod yaml 配置文件，它看起来不错。 我认为它是由 Gitpod 预构建的，所以不可能有语法错误。

但是，我有一个建议。 我查看了 .gitpod.yml 的文档，注意到它们支持 VSCode 扩展建议。 我还去了我们项目中的.vscode/extensions.json ，因为通常会在此处指定推荐的扩展名，并找到了一个 - “amodio.tsl-problem-matcher”。 所以，我建议也将它包含在 Gitpod 中。

整体体验🙌

总的来说，站在拉取请求的另一边是非常有帮助的经历。 我了解了新工具，并利用我以前经验中的现有知识来帮助做出更多质的改变。



原文:

Today, I reviewed multiple Pull Requests that were made to our internal class projects. I wanted to contribute to one of the newer ones, so I decided to start with Seneca's VSCode extension. Plus, I really liked the idea of what it can become.

1️⃣ First pull request

Link: https://github.com/Seneca-CDOT/vscode-seneca-college/pull/13

The first pull request that I found was made by @IvaniGabrovsky and it included changes related to the creation of the CONTRIBUTING.md and README.md files.

CONTRIBUTING.md's purpose is to have instructions for the contributors and an overview of the project to help them get started with the changes. Meanwhile, README.md's primary audience are the users and it should tell what the tool is, how to use it and any additional helpful info. It also serves as the first stop point when learning about the project.

Ivan made a great job, however there were many places for improvement, some of which were already outlined by the professor, @humphd, and another student, @SerpentBytes.

I decided to still take a look. I started a review after giving my first comment and ended it with the request for changes.

I focused on multiple areas such as:

  1. grammar, as these files represent the project and cannot have big grammar mistakes (influences reputation);
  2. wording and content, as I have read through many similar files for other projects, I can tell what are some important steps and can give suggestions;
  3. guide for other comments, as Ivan sometimes asked for help through personal messages, I wanted to guide him where I can and give some recommendations for previously mentioned issues.

Ivan responded to every suggestion very quickly and made all corresponding changes.

2️⃣ Second pull request

Link: https://github.com/Seneca-CDOT/vscode-seneca-college/pull/12

I continued to review pull requests in the same project and found this one, which was made by @P-DR0ZD and focused on setting up Gitpod for the project. It created main Gitpod files and added information about Gitpod setup to the README.md.

First, I had to research about Gitpod, as I do not have much experience about it and in order to test it I need to figure out what is expected. After some googling and looking through its documentation, I figured out that it allows to setup development environments to work on the projects online which is very helpful to get contribution going.

First, I check the link provided in the new README.md file and it worked perfectly. I could open VSCode through Gitpod and the project would be automatically prepared for me.

I also checked out new Gitpod yaml configuration file which looked good to me. I think it was prebuilt by Gitpod, so no possible syntax mistakes.

However, I had one suggestion. I looked through the documentation for .gitpod.yml and noticed that they have support for VSCode extension recommendations. I also went to .vscode/extensions.json in our project, as it is where recommended extensions would usually be specified, and found one - "amodio.tsl-problem-matcher". So, I suggested to include it for Gitpod as well.

Overall experience 🙌

Overall, it was very helpful experience to get on the other side of pull requests. I learned about new tools and used my existing knowledge from my previous experiences to help make more qualitative changes.

Items.Title: Typescript `satisfies never`：Typescript 4.9 中的详尽检查 | Typescript `satisfies never`: Exhaustiveness checking in Typescript 4.9
Items.Description:
很长一段时间以来，我一直在为 Typescript 的新satisfies运算符辩护，很高兴看到它出现在 4.9 的语言中。

本文介绍了satisfies的一个具体且有价值的用例。 如果你需要算子的背景介绍，可以看我在 Medium 上关于 satisfies 的文章。

用例：处理联合的每个案例

处理联合的每种情况是 Typescript 代码库中所需的常见模式。 这可能是一个简单的“字符串文字”联合，例如...

 type Flavour = " vanilla " | " chocolate " | " mint " ;

在可区分联合中，对象的命名属性用于区分对象可以采用的不同状态聚合。 运行时的代码路径会使用相等性检查，但 Typescript 可以在编译时推理这些代码路径，帮助我们消除错误。

示例：“技术裁员”游戏

作为一个可行的示例，让我们在一个假想的“技术裁员”游戏中定义一个用户操作。

在游戏中，你首先要在办公室里跑来跑去，试图找到今天被迫进入办公室的同事，并在他们被立即解雇之前与他们道别。

 type Action = | { kind : " runaround " ; speed : number ; } | { kind : " saygoodbye " ; phrase : " Hasta la vista " | " I'll be back " ; }

如果没有想到别的，我会使用kind作为区分属性。 每个动作结构都有不同的有效负载，因此您不能编写访问"runaround"绕行”动作的phrase属性或"saygoodbye"动作的speed属性的代码。

事实上，联合本身既没有speed也没有phrase属性，因为这些属性并没有在联合的所有成员上定义。 让我们编写一个动作处理程序，它利用共同的属性，并允许 Typescript 推断我们正在处理哪种动作。

 function handleAction ( action : Action ) { const { kind } = action ; if ( kind === " runaround " ) { setSpeed ( action . speed ); return ; } if ( kind === " saygoodbye " ) { say ( action . phrase ); return ; } }

它处理所有情况 - 你检查过。 两条路径都编译并且可以访问数字或字符串以分别调用setSpeed和say 。 到目前为止，一切都很好。

但是，对于企业代码库来说，它确实不够好。 您希望贡献者能够在 2 年后进行更改，而不必跟踪每个可能的隐含错误。

失败案例

游戏设计师从“Tech Layoffs”的玩家那里得到了非常明确的用户反馈。 用户知道规则，他们知道游戏，他们会玩。 但老实说，他们不喜欢在没有灵魂的迷宫般的小隔间里无休止地奔跑和告别已故同事的循环。

作为新功能的工作，游戏中添加了退出旅程。 该类型被扩展为"giveup"案例......

 type Action = | { kind : " runaround " ; speed : number ; } | { kind : " saygoodbye " ; phrase : " Hasta la vista " | " I'll be back " ; } | { kind : " giveup " ; reason : " Too difficult " | " Made me cry " ; }

但是......哦不，我们的处理程序没有进行相应的更改。 它默默地忽略了游戏中的一个动作，而且这个变化没有引发编译器错误！

让编译器强制执行处理程序的详尽性不是很好吗？ 好吧，自 Typescript 4.9 以来，有一种非常优雅的方式！

下面的示例显示了一个处理程序，它会在任何人向未处理的联合添加案例时立即中断，只需添加行kind satisfies never （并且可能是有用的评论）。

 function handleAction ( action : Action ) { const { kind } = action ; if ( kind === " runaround " ) { setSpeed ( action . speed ); return ; } if ( kind === " saygoodbye " ) { say ( action . phrase ); return ; } // every `kind` already handled // line should never be hit kind satisfies never ; }

第一个if子句以这种方式消除了"runaround" 。 第二个淘汰了"saygoodbye" 。 如果每个值都被消除，那么kind的值确实可以分配给 type never 。 也就是说，控制流分析已经​​消除了这条路径—— kind路径再也没有价值了。 这个处理程序永远不会让你失望。

如何知道发生了什么？

在这里，您可以在编辑器中看到编译器错误的显示。 由于我们添加了一个额外的操作，它会警告您可能有kind值可能会到达警卫处。

最后修复很简单 - 确保详尽无遗使红线消失......

你不会得到这个与任何其他人

对于那些读到这里的人，我愿意工作，你可以在https://cefn.com/cv查看我的简历



原文:

I've made the case for Typescript's new satisfies operator for a long time, and was really pleased to see it land in the language in 4.9.

This article introduces a specific and valuable use case for satisfies. If you need a background introduction to the operator, you can see my Medium article on satisfies .

Use Case: Handle every case of a union

Handling every case of a union is a common pattern needed in a Typescript codebase. This could be a simple 'string literal' union like...

type Flavour = "vanilla" | "chocolate" | "mint";

...or a more complex discriminated union as per the worked example below.

In a discriminated union a named property of an object is used to differentiate between distinct aggregates of state that an object can take. Code paths at runtime will use equality checks, but Typescript can reason about these code paths at compile time and help eliminate mistakes for us.

Example: 'Tech Layoffs' Game

As a worked example, let's define a user action in an imaginary 'Tech Layoffs' game.

In the game you first have to run around the office, trying to find any co-workers who have been forced to come into the office today, and say goodbye to them before they are summarily fired.

type Action = | { kind: "runaround"; speed: number; } | { kind: "saygoodbye"; phrase: "Hasta la vista" | "I'll be back"; }

I use kind as a discriminating property if nothing else comes to mind. The word type already has a meaning in Typescript.

Each action structure has a distinct payload, so you can't write code that accesses the phrase property of a "runaround" action or the speed property of a "saygoodbye" action.

In fact the union itself has neither speed nor phrase properties, because those properties are not defined on all members of the union. The kind property is the only one in common.

Let's write an action handler which exploits the property in common, and allows Typescript to reason about which kind of action we are dealing with.

function handleAction(action: Action) { const { kind } = action; if (kind === "runaround") { setSpeed(action.speed); return; } if (kind === "saygoodbye") { say(action.phrase); return; }
}

It handles all the cases - you checked. Both paths compile and can access a number or string to call setSpeed and say respectively. So far so good.

However, it really isn't good enough for an enterprise codebase. You want contributors to be able to make changes 2 years from now without having to trace every possible implicit bug.

The failure case

The game designer is getting really clear user feedback from the players of "Tech Layoffs". The users know the rules, they know the game and they're going to play it. But honestly they don't like the endless cycle of fearful running and saying farewell to departed colleagues around a soulless maze of cubicles.

As new feature work, an exit journey is added to the game. The type was extended with a "giveup" case...

type Action = | { kind: "runaround"; speed: number; } | { kind: "saygoodbye"; phrase: "Hasta la vista" | "I'll be back"; } | { kind: "giveup"; reason: "Too difficult" | "Made me cry"; }

But...oh no, the corresponding change wasn't made to our handler. It's silently ignoring one of the Actions in the game, and the change raised no compiler errors!

Wouldn't it be nice to have the handler's exhaustiveness enforced by the compiler. Well since Typescript 4.9 there is a really elegant way!

The example below shows a handler that breaks as soon as anyone adds a case to the union which isn't handled, by simply adding the line kind satisfies never (and maybe a helpful comment).

function handleAction(action: Action) { const { kind } = action; if (kind === "runaround") { setSpeed(action.speed); return; } if (kind === "saygoodbye") { say(action.phrase); return; } // every `kind` already handled // line should never be hit kind satisfies never;
}

Typescript can reason about the control flow as our procedure eliminates values from the union because we return when any value is matched.

The first if clause eliminated "runaround" in this way. The second eliminated "saygoodbye". The line kind satisfies never then acts as a guard that there are no further values.

If every value has been eliminated then the value of kind can indeed be assigned to type never. That is to say the control flow analysis has eliminated that path - there's no further value that kind could ever be. This handler is never going to let you down.

How to know what's been going on?

Here you can see the presentation of the compiler error in an editor. Since we added an extra action, it warns you that there's a possible value of kind that might reach the guard.

Finally the fix is easy - ensuring exhaustiveness makes the redline go away...

You wouldn't get this with any other guy

For those who read this far, I'm open to work, and you can see my CV at https://cefn.com/cv

Items.Title: 如何使用这两个技巧氧化公共 RESTful JSON API | How to oxidize a public RESTful JSON API with these two tips
Items.Description:
使用curlconverter和转换工具将公共 API 转换为软件库； 此示例介绍了如何使用带有mempool.space的 rust 来做到这一点。

本文将介绍如何使用 mempool.space API 并将其转换为一些 Rust 代码。 什么是内存池空间？ 这是一个实现 API 以访问比特币区块链数据的网站。 该 API 实现了一个“esplora”风格的后端，有助于标准化比特币的区块链访问，这有助于分散网络，因为应用程序开发人员只能实现这种访问风格，并且区块链提供商有无数的选择。

在许多方面，这是一个出色的 API，因为它通过命令行将比特币区块链和内存池的低级丑陋变成了具有精美图形的高级界面。 这对于希望以低成本与比特币协议进行交互的任何人都非常有用。

本教程将适用于任何其他公共 API，如果您有不同的需求，请继续阅读！

我的主要动机是我有一个比特币钱包，我想在其中添加费用建议。

这高度评价了它的质量。

  • 类型安全和 SerDe crate； 任何时候你接触到一个公共 API，你都应该非常偏执并验证一切。 货运工具很棒
  • 如果您不知道比特币内存池是什么，请阅读 mempool.space常见问题解答或询问您当地的比特币。 这是比特币协议的一个迷人方面（充满了它自己的争议），对于那些对分布式系统感到好奇的人来说尤其有趣！

    A mempool (short for "memory pool") is the queue of pending and unconfirmed transactions for a cryptocurrency network node. There is no one global mempool: every node on the network maintains its own mempool, so different nodes may hold different transactions in their mempools.

    为此，您需要知道如何调用 API 以及响应是什么。 如果没有文档或无法访问 API，此工作流程将不会有效。

    为简洁起见，我将展示三个 API 分组的示例：费用、一般和内存池。

    稍后，我想做一个关于这个的系列，包括：

    • 添加异步
    • 使用构建器模式配置 API crate
    • 功能保护（针对 API 的不同部分）
    • 您认为有用或有趣的任何其他内容 - 请在下方、gh 存储库中或直接通过电子邮件或其他方式告诉我。

    我们现在也将坚持使用测试网，这将是一个非常简单的箱子，没有配置选项。 稍后我们将添加网络配置。

    所以如果我们想要调整难度，文档告诉我们运行；

     $ curl -sSL "https://mempool.space/testnet/api/v1/difficulty-adjustment"

    所以如果我们运行它，我们会得到；

     $ curl -sSL "https://mempool.space/testnet/api/v1/difficulty-adjustment" { "progressPercent" :83.53174603174604, "difficultyChange" :7.02698236660555, "estimatedRetargetDate" :1668895091192, "remainingBlocks" :332, "remainingTime" :186121192, "previousRetarget" :247.24200134108503, "nextRetargetHeight" :2407104, "timeAvg" :560606, "timeOffset" :0 } %

    这对阅读来说不是很好，让我们通过jq运行它；

     $ curl -sSL "https://mempool.space/testnet/api/v1/difficulty-adjustment" | jq { "progressPercent" : 83.58134920634922, "difficultyChange" : 7.082824486908046, "estimatedRetargetDate" : 1668894501603, "remainingBlocks" : 331, "remainingTime" : 185463603, "previousRetarget" : 247.24200134108503, "nextRetargetHeight" : 2407104, "timeAvg" : 560313, "timeOffset" : 0 }

    所以我们想在 Rust 代码中模拟这个功能，该怎么做呢？

    好吧，我们需要创建一个函数来获取该 API 端点并调用它，然后获取响应并反序列化通过线路传入的任何内容。 好的！ 所以我们需要一个函数。 我们如何将 curl 变成一个 rust 函数？

    我们可以使用curlconverter.com

    这是example.com的示例


     extern crate reqwest ; fn main () -> Result < (), Box < dyn std :: error :: Error >> { let client = reqwest :: blocking :: Client :: new (); let res = client .get ( "https://mempool.space/testnet/api/v1/difficulty-adjustment" ) .send () ? .text () ? ; println! ( "{}" , res ); Ok (()) }

    我已经将该代码转换为replit

    好的，我们有了函数，但让我们来看看如何将 json 响应转换为 rust 结构。

     { progressPercent: 44.397234501112074 , difficultyChange: 98.45932018381687 , estimatedRetargetDate: 1627762478 , remainingBlocks: 1121 , remainingTime: 665977 , previousRetarget: -4.807005268478962 , nextRetargetHeight: 741888 , timeAvg: 302328 , timeOffset: 0 }

    要将它变成一个 rust 结构，我们可以使用它
    变压器工具

    这是基本的例子，希望这也很清楚我要去哪里。

    您必须粘贴 GET 请求的回复，在我的系统上我使用 pbcopy 就像这样

    $ curl "https://mempool.space/testnet/api/v1/difficulty-adjustment" | jq | pbcopy

    您必须验证类型是否已正确转换，不要相信该工具。 首先，这对我来说看起来是正确的。 您可以随时返回并将语义上更合适的类型交换到库中，基本类型现在就可以了。 因为这些只是数字，所以它可能很好，但是任何加密原语都应该通过使用适当的类型来验证。 您还可以添加 unix 时间戳时间。

     use reqwest ; use serde_derive :: Deserialize ; use serde_derive :: Serialize ; #[derive(Default, Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)] #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase" )] pub struct DifficultyAdjustment { pub progress_percent : f64 , pub difficulty_change : f64 , pub estimated_retarget_date : i64 , pub remaining_blocks : i64 , pub remaining_time : i64 , pub previous_retarget : f64 , pub next_retarget_height : i64 , pub time_avg : i64 , pub time_offset : i64 , } fn main () -> Result < (), Box < dyn std :: error :: Error >> { let client = reqwest :: blocking :: Client :: new (); let res = client .get ( "https://mempool.space/testnet/api/v1/difficulty-adjustment" ) .send () ? .text () ? ; println! ( "{}" , res ); Ok (()) }

    不要忘记添加依赖项，cargo 有一个扩展可以使这变得容易；

     $ cargo add serde serde_derive serde_json reqwest -F blocking

    通过使用 json serde 函数，我们可以让 crate 自动生成所有 json 文本解析器，而不是手动完成。 如果任何东西解析失败，就会出现一个错误，并且由于它没有被处理，我们的程序就会崩溃。

     use reqwest ; use serde_derive :: Deserialize ; use serde_derive :: Serialize ; #[derive(Default, Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)] #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase" )] pub struct DifficultyAdjustment { pub progress_percent : f64 , pub difficulty_change : f64 , pub estimated_retarget_date : i64 , pub remaining_blocks : i64 , pub remaining_time : i64 , pub previous_retarget : f64 , pub next_retarget_height : i64 , pub time_avg : i64 , pub time_offset : i64 , } fn main () -> Result < (), Box < dyn std :: error :: Error >> { let client = reqwest :: blocking :: Client :: new (); let res = client .get ( "https://mempool.space/testnet/api/v1/difficulty-adjustment" ) .send () ? .text () ? ; println! ( "text: {}" , res ); let val = serde_json :: from_str :: < DifficultyAdjustment > ( & res ) .expect ( "could not parse json" ); println! ( "whole struct: {:?}" , val ); println! ( "just the difficulty change: {:?}" , val .difficulty_change ); Ok (()) }

    这就是基本功能，在接下来的帖子中，我将把它变成一个库。 敬请关注！



    • 原文:

    TLDR;
    Use curlconverter and transform tools to turn a public API into a software library; this example covers how to do it using rust with mempool.space.

    This article will go over how to take the mempool.space API and turn it into some rust code. What is mempool.space? It's a website that implements an API to access bitcoin's blockchain data. The API implements an "esplora" style backend which helps to standardize bitcoin's blockchain access which helps to decentralize the network since app devs can only implement this access style and have a myriad of choices for blockchain provider.

    Mempool.space is widely accepted by many in the bitcoin community, mainly because it shows all the most essential information about the bitcoin mempool at-a-glance through a stunning interface. In many ways, this is an excellent API because it turns the low-level ugliness of the bitcoin blockchain and mempool through the command line into a high-level interface with beautiful graphics. This is useful for anyone that wishes to interact with the bitcoin protocol at a low cost.

    This tutorial will apply to any other public API, so read on if you have different needs!

    My primary motivation is that I have a bitcoin wallet to which I want to add fee recommendations.

    Rust is the most appropriate tool for this task because;

    • The http crate, hyper, is an optional backend for cURL. This speaks highly of its quality.
    • Type safety and the SerDe crate; anytime you reach out to a public API you should be very paranoid and verify everything. - Enforcing invariants is easy with rust. SerDe makes "Don't trust, verify" convenient to implement.
    • The cargo tool is great
    • C calling convention, so FFI with typescript and python is theoretically possible

    If you don't know what the bitcoin mempool is, then read the mempool.space FAQ or ask your local bitcoiner. It is a fascinating aspect of the bitcoin protocol (replete with its own controversy), its especially interesting to those who are curious about distributed systems!

    A mempool (short for "memory pool") is the queue of pending and unconfirmed transactions for a cryptocurrency network node. There is no one global mempool: every node on the network maintains its own mempool, so different nodes may hold different transactions in their mempools.

    For this to work, you need to know how to invoke the API and what the responses are. Without docs or access to the API, this workflow will not be effective.

    For brevity, I will showcase the example for three API groupings, fees, general and mempool.

    Later on, I would like to make a series about this, including;

    • Adding async
    • Using a builder pattern to configure the API crate
    • Feature guards (for different parts of the API)
    • Rust's FFI for typescript and python
    • Anything else you think would be useful or interesting - please let me know below, in the gh repo, or directly via email or some other means.

    We will also stick to testnet for now, this will be a very simple crate with no config options. We will add network configuration later.

    So if we want the difficulty adjustment, the docs tell us to run;

    $ curl -sSL "https://mempool.space/testnet/api/v1/difficulty-adjustment"

    So if we run that, we would get;

    $ curl -sSL "https://mempool.space/testnet/api/v1/difficulty-adjustment"
{"progressPercent":83.53174603174604,"difficultyChange":7.02698236660555,"estimatedRetargetDate":1668895091192,"remainingBlocks":332,"remainingTime":186121192,"previousRetarget":247.24200134108503,"nextRetargetHeight":2407104,"timeAvg":560606,"timeOffset":0}%

    which is not so great for reading, lets run that through jq;

    $ curl -sSL "https://mempool.space/testnet/api/v1/difficulty-adjustment" | jq
{ "progressPercent": 83.58134920634922, "difficultyChange": 7.082824486908046, "estimatedRetargetDate": 1668894501603, "remainingBlocks": 331, "remainingTime": 185463603, "previousRetarget": 247.24200134108503, "nextRetargetHeight": 2407104, "timeAvg": 560313, "timeOffset": 0
}

    So we want to emulate this functionality in rust code, how would be do that?

    Well we need to make a function that takes that API endpoint and calls it, then takes the response and deserialzes whatever comes in through the wire. Curl does these things for us, but we can't easily call curl from our programs (well you can almost always have your app run a subprocess that invokes curl and reads from a file and manually parses it but that is less than ideal for a variety of reasons, namely security and sanity).

    Ok! so we need a function. How do we turn curl into a rust function?

    We can use curlconverter.com

    Here is an example for example.com 


    extern crate reqwest; fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> { let client = reqwest::blocking::Client::new(); let res = client.get("https://mempool.space/testnet/api/v1/difficulty-adjustment").send()? .text()?; println!("{}", res); Ok(()) }

    I've converted that code into a replit

    Ok so we have the function, but lets go over how to turn the json response into a rust struct.

    { progressPercent: 44.397234501112074, difficultyChange: 98.45932018381687, estimatedRetargetDate: 1627762478, remainingBlocks: 1121, remainingTime: 665977, previousRetarget: -4.807005268478962, nextRetargetHeight: 741888, timeAvg: 302328, timeOffset: 0}

    To turn this into a rust struct, we can use this
    transformer.tools

    ![[Pasted image 20221117130709.png]]

    Here is the basic example, hopefully this is also clear where I am going with this.

    You have to paste in the reply from the GET request, on my system I use pbcopy like so

    $ curl "https://mempool.space/testnet/api/v1/difficulty-adjustment" | jq | pbcopy

    You must verify that the types are properly transformed, dont trust the tool. This looks correct to me, to start with. You can always go back and swap in more semantically appropriate types to the library, primitive types will do for now. Since these are only numbers, its probably fine as is, but any cryptographic primitives shoud be verified by using the appropriate types. You could also add the unix timestamp time.

    use reqwest;
use serde_derive::Deserialize;
use serde_derive::Serialize; #[derive(Default, Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct DifficultyAdjustment { pub progress_percent: f64, pub difficulty_change: f64, pub estimated_retarget_date: i64, pub remaining_blocks: i64, pub remaining_time: i64, pub previous_retarget: f64, pub next_retarget_height: i64, pub time_avg: i64, pub time_offset: i64,
} fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> { let client = reqwest::blocking::Client::new(); let res = client.get("https://mempool.space/testnet/api/v1/difficulty-adjustment") .send()? .text()?; println!("{}", res); Ok(())
}

    Don't forget to add dependencies, cargo has an extension that makes this easy;

    $ cargo add serde serde_derive serde_json reqwest -F blocking

    By using the json serde function we can get the crate to automatically generate all the json text parsers, rather than doing it by hand. If anything fails to parse, an error bubbles up and since its not handled, our program panics.

    use reqwest;
use serde_derive::Deserialize;
use serde_derive::Serialize; #[derive(Default, Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct DifficultyAdjustment { pub progress_percent: f64, pub difficulty_change: f64, pub estimated_retarget_date: i64, pub remaining_blocks: i64, pub remaining_time: i64, pub previous_retarget: f64, pub next_retarget_height: i64, pub time_avg: i64, pub time_offset: i64,
} fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> { let client = reqwest::blocking::Client::new(); let res = client .get("https://mempool.space/testnet/api/v1/difficulty-adjustment") .send()? .text()?; println!("text: {}", res); let val = serde_json::from_str::<DifficultyAdjustment>(&res).expect("could not parse json"); println!("whole struct: {:?}", val); println!("just the difficulty change: {:?}", val.difficulty_change); Ok(())
}

    Thats pretty much it for basic functionality, in the next posts, I will turn this into a library. Stay tuned!

安舒137

发表于本实验室专注于此主题

  • 软件发布的一般概念和术语
  • 包管理器
  • 用户测试

释放工具

为了发布我的应用程序，我使用了npm 。

要发布您的软件，您可以按照以下步骤操作。

如果根目录中没有，请创建一个package.json文件。

使用此 cmd 创建新的 package.json 文件npm init 。

一个 package.json 文件：

  • 列出你的项目所依赖的包
  • 使用语义版本控制规则指定您的项目可以使用的包版本
  • 使您的构建可重现，因此更容易与其他开发人员共享
  • 确保在 package.json 文件中添加name 、 version和author 。

将 README.md 文件添加到包中

  • 在文本编辑器的包根目录中，创建一个名为 README.md 的文件。
  • 在 README.md 文件中，添加有关您的包的有用信息。
  • 保存 README.md 文件。

发布你的版本

  • 确保您已经创建了npm帐户并进行了验证。
  • 在您的终端上使用 cmd npm login 。 使用您的username和password 。
  • 如果要创建和发布范围内的公共包，请使用命令npm publish --access public 。

发布我的静态站点以将其本地安装在您的设备上后，您可以使用此 cmd npm i agandhi29_static_site_geneator

This lab focus on this topic

  • General concepts and terminology of software releases
  • Git tags
  • Package managers
  • User testing

Release tool

To release my app I have used npm.

To release your software, you can follow these steps.

Create a package.json file if you don't have in the root directory.

Use this cmd to create the new package.json file npm init.

A package.json file:

  • Lists the packages your project depends on
  • Specifies versions of a package that your project can use using semantic versioning rules
  • Makes your build reproducible, and therefore easier to share with other developers
  • Make sure to add the name, version and author in your package.json file.

ADD a README.md file to a package

  • In a text editor, in your package root directory, create a file called README.md.
  • In the README.md file, add useful information about your package.
  • Save the README.md file.

Publish your release

  • Make sure you have created the account npm and verified it.
  • Use the cmd npm login on your terminal. Login with your username and password.
  • Use the command npm publish --access public if you want to create and publish scoped public packages.

Items.Title: 协作的重要性 | The Importance Of Collaborating
Items.Description:
桑特埃文斯

发表于我们都会表现出虚假的自信，或者表现得好像我们知道自己在做什么，但事实并非如此。 在 Flatiron 进入第一阶段三天后，我学到了一些不同的课程。 我可以毫无疑问地说，这使我免于在成为软件工程师的旅程中提前退出。

了解 Javascript 的基础知识，例如变量、数组、对象和箭头函数，让我的大脑在做夏盐。 随着实验室的进行，我忍不住想到会落后并且吃得太多了。

 **const filmBox = document.querySelector("#film-box"); const likes = document.querySelector("#like-count"); const EMPTY_HEART = "♡"; const FULL_HEART = "♥";**

第一次遇到“const”和“let”时，我不知道该用什么，什么时候用，为什么用。 查看提供的材料并使用 google 和我现在最喜欢的 w3schools 信息网站等资源，我仍然发现自己迷路了。 在这一刻，我只是屈服了，做了本该做的事情来避免让自己头疼……承认我需要帮助。 我收起我的骄傲，问我的同龄人。 来发现他们是一样的。 精确的。 船。 出乎意料地迷茫和迷茫。

我从第一阶段获得的最有价值的教训是几个大脑总比一个好。 编码就是其中之一，它是一个永无止境的学习之旅。 自然地，您不会知道所有事情，这是不可能的，但很棒的是，如果您不知道，有人会更好地理解。 例如，与我的同事一起工作时，我们将“const”和“let”分解为我们可以理解的最简单的方式。 两者都可以在您的代码中的任何地方使用，没有它们将无法正常工作。 但是，将两者分开的是一旦“const”分配了一个变量，它就不能重新声明或重新分配。 至于'let'，必须在使用前声明一个变量。

相反，它是一位导师、同事或同事让你自己站出来寻求帮助。 并非所有的战斗都需要一个人去征服。

原文:

SonteEvans

Posted on Nov 18

We're all guilty of portraying false confidence or acting like we know what we're doing even though that's not the case. Three days into Phase One at Flatiron I learned a few different lessons. That I can say without a doubt saved me from calling it early quits on my journey of becoming a Software Engineer.

Understanding the fundamentals of Javascript such as variables, arrays, objects, and arrow functions had my brain doing summer salts. Following along with labs I couldn't help the thought of falling behind and biting off more than I could chew.

**const filmBox = document.querySelector("#film-box");
const likes = document.querySelector("#like-count");
const EMPTY_HEART = "♡";
const FULL_HEART = "♥";**

When encountering 'const and 'let' for the first time I couldn't wrap my head around which to use, when, and why. Reviewing material provided and using resources such as google and my now favorite website for information w3schools, I still found myself lost. At this moment I just gave in and did what should have been done to save myself the headache...admit I needed help. I swallowed my pride and asked my fellow peers. Come to find out they were in the same. exact. boat. Lost and confused surprisingly.

The most valuable lesson I gained from Phase One was several brains are better than one. Coding is one of those things it's a never-ending journey of learning. Naturally, you won't know everything it's impossible but what's great is that if you don't know someone will have a better understanding. For example, working with my peers we broke 'const' and 'let' down to the easiest way we could understand. Both can be used anywhere within your code and won't work properly without them. However, what separates the two is once 'const' has a variable assigned it cannot be redeclared nor reassigned. As for 'let' a variable must be declared before use.

Rather it is an instructor, peer, or co-worker put yourself out there and ask for help. Not all battles have to be conquered alone.
